headline: EMCOR (EME) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: In the first quarter, we generated revenues of $4.63 billion, representing year-over-year growth of 19.7% and organic growth of 16.8% when adjusting for incremental acquisition contribution and the sale of EMCOR U.K.  Operating income reached $404 million, with 8.7% operating margin, while diluted earnings per share of $6.84 represents an increase of 30% versus the first quarter of 2025.  This reflects our strategic positioning in high-growth markets and operational excellence across our construction and services platforms.

This reflects our strategic positioning in high-growth markets and operational excellence across our construction and services platforms.

headline: EMCOR Group Inc (NYSE:EME) Q1 2026 Beat Boosts Full-Year Guidance
summary: EMCOR Group smashed Q1 2026 estimates with $4.63B revenue and $6.84 EPS, driven by record backlog and strong demand. Management raised full-year guidance, signaling robust growth ahead.

headline: EMCOR (NYSE:EME) Surprises With Strong Q1 CY2026
summary: Specialty construction contractor company EMCOR (NYSE:EME) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 19.7% year on year to $4.63 billion. The company’s full-year revenue guidance of $18.88 billion at the midpoint came in 4.1% above analysts’ estimates. Its GAAP profit of $6.84 per share was 17.1% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: EMCOR Refocuses On U.S. Growth As It Exits U.K. Operations
summary: EMCOR Group (NYSE:EME) has agreed to sell its U.K. Building Services operations, aiming to increase financial flexibility for future growth initiatives. The transaction reshapes the company’s portfolio by exiting a non-U.S. segment and concentrating resources on areas management views as core. Management has indicated that proceeds from the sale are intended for growth-focused uses rather than short-term cash retention.
